name: Blackwing - Chinook the Snow Blast
name_jp: <ruby lang="jp"><rb>ＢＦ</rb><rp> (</rp><rt>ブラックフェザー</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby>－<ruby lang="jp"><rb>雪</rb><rp> (</rp><rt>せつ</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby><ruby lang="jp"><rb>撃</rb><rp> (</rp><rt>げき</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby>のチヌーク
name_jp_tl: Black Feather - Chinook the Snow Blast
desc: You can send this card from your hand or field to the GY, then target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; send 1 "Blackwing" Synchro Monster or 1 "Black-Winged Dragon" from your Extra Deck to the GY, and if you do, until the end of this turn, the targeted monster loses 700 ATK, also negate its effects. This is a Quick Effect if you control a DARK Synchro Monster. You can only use this effect of "Blackwing - Chinook the Snow Blast" once per turn.
mnst_eff: You can send this card from your hand or field to the GY, then target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; send 1 "Blackwing" Synchro Monster or 1 "Black-Winged Dragon" from your Extra Deck to the GY, and if you do, until the end of this turn, the targeted monster loses 700 ATK, also negate its effects. This is a Quick Effect if you control a DARK Synchro Monster. You can only use this effect of "Blackwing - Chinook the Snow Blast" once per turn.
eff: You can send this card from your hand or field to the GY, then target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; send 1 "Blackwing" Synchro Monster or 1 "Black-Winged Dragon" from your Extra Deck to the GY, and if you do, until the end of this turn, the targeted monster loses 700 ATK, also negate its effects. This is a Quick Effect if you control a DARK Synchro Monster. You can only use this effect of "Blackwing - Chinook the Snow Blast" once per turn.

sets.set: 25th Anniversary Tin: Dueling Heroes Mega Pack

action: Sends itself from hand to Graveyard for cost
Sends itself from field to Graveyard for cost
Sends from your Extra Deck to your Graveyard
mst: Negates the effects of your opponent's Effect Monsters
misc: Requires control of specific Monster card type
Can be activated during either player's turn
